layman_headline: Market fear and geopolitical tensions make this a bad time to chase — staying on the sidelines.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is pulling back due to U.S.-Iran tensions near the Strait of Hormuz, and most altcoins are in clear downtrends. The Fear & Greed Index sits at 28 (Fear), which is improving from last week's 24.6, but not yet at the extreme levels that historically produce reliable bounce trades. The candidates on our watchlist either already ran up 20-30% and look extended, or have bearish chart structures with poor risk/reward ratios.
layman_action: Wait patiently. Do not buy into this weakness yet. If Fear & Greed drops below 20 or we see a clear capitulation flush, that would be a much better entry point. Keep cash ready for that moment.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate fails the quality filter for different reasons: KITE and VELVET have run 17-26% in 7 days and are extended with bearish chart bias (-1.4 and -0.3). LIT is RSI 72 overbought after a 55% 5-day ramp. JTO has decent structure (bias +0.4) but RSI 59 is elevated and R/R is only 1.26x. ETHFI has the best chart bias (+0.6) but resistance is only 2.5% away making R/R a terrible 0.11x. GRASS is down 21% in 7 days but shows a bearish engulfing pattern and -0.9 bias. DCR's RSI 19 is compelling but the bounce already started and price is below all MAs. With BTC dominance at 56.1% (hostile to alts), geopolitical risk dragging BTC below $63K, and no volume breakout signals among candidates, the highest-probability play is patience.
risk_factors: U.S.-Iran Hormuz tensions could escalate further, pushing BTC below $60K and dragging all alts down 15-25%
BTC dominance at 56.1% means capital is flowing away from altcoins — any alt position faces structural headwinds
Fear & Greed at 28 is improving but not yet at extreme fear (<15) where contrarian entries have the highest win rates
watch_list: BTC price action around $62K support — a break below $60K would create deeper alt capitulation and better entries
Fear & Greed Index — watch for a drop below 20 which would signal extreme fear and a higher-probability bounce zone
DCR and ETHFI — if DCR retests $11 support with RSI still oversold, or ETHFI breaks above $0.40 resistance with volume, those become actionable setups
market_context: Crypto markets are under pressure from U.S.-Iran geopolitical tensions near the Strait of Hormuz, with BTC falling below $63K despite continued ETF inflows. High BTC dominance (56.1%) signals that altcoins are particularly vulnerable in this risk-off environment.
context_banner: Geopolitical risk and high BTC dominance create hostile conditions for altcoin swing trades — patience is the highest-conviction play right now.

key_signals.description: Resurgent U.S.-Iran hostilities are the primary driver of crypto weakness, with markets pricing in only 3% chance of Hormuz traffic normalizing by August — a persistent overhang.
Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) is improving from 24.6 weekly average, but hasn't reached extreme fear levels (<20) where our 80% win-rate contrarian entries historically trigger.
DCR's RSI of 19.6 is deeply oversold (strongest signal among candidates), but the 23% bounce already in progress reduces the remaining upside from current levels.
BTC bull market potentially delayed to September per analysis, and institutional tokenization momentum (UK taskforce, SBI/Solana) is constructive long-term but doesn't help short-term alt trades.
No candidate showed positive chart bias above +0.6, and the best-structured coins (JTO, ETHFI) had poor risk/reward ratios (1.26x and 0.11x respectively) — no clean setups available.
